title: Heather Pringle on the upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of a major space organization, discusses upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space on the Space Minds podcast. The interview highlights her insights as a retired US Air Force Major General.

title: China conducts pair of Long March launches for Thousand Sails and Guowang megaconstellations
summary: China launched two Long March rockets (Long March 8 and Long March 6A) this week, deploying new satellites for the Thousand Sails and Guowang megaconstellation projects.

title: A new kind of arsenal: commercial satellites
summary: The Pentagon is shifting its strategy to rely on commercial satellites for future conflicts, recognizing their growing importance. This marks a significant shift in military space operations.

title: What returning to lunar space means for human health
summary: Returning to the lunar surface poses significant health risks for astronauts due to prolonged exposure to spaceflight hazards, some of which were less understood during the Apollo missions. The upcoming lunar missions aim to address these challenges after decades of limited human presence beyond low Earth orbit.

title: Portal Space Systems raises $50 million to accelerate spacecraft development
summary: Portal Space Systems, a space mobility company, secured $50 million in funding to advance the development of its highly maneuverable spacecraft. The investment aims to accelerate the company's growth and technological progress in the space sector.

title: Space Force taps 14 firms for $1.8 billion GEO surveillance program
summary: The U.S. Space Force has selected 14 companies to compete for $1.8 billion in contracts to develop 'neighborhood watch' satellites for GEO surveillance. These contracts aim to enhance space domain awareness in geostationary orbit.

title: Albedo prepares second VLEO mission for 2027 launch
summary: Albedo, a satellite manufacturing company, announced plans to launch its second spacecraft, Vicinity, into very low-Earth orbit (VLEO) in 2027. The new mission aims to deploy advanced solar arrays for increased power compared to its previous 2025 launch.

title: Lockheed Martin wins $105 million contract for GPS ground control system as OCX winds down
summary: Lockheed Martin secured a $105 million contract to develop a GPS ground control system for next-generation GPS IIIF satellites, replacing the aging OCX system. The deal extends support through 2030.

title: Vantor to expand imaging satellite fleet, adding smallsats to increase revisit rates
summary: Vantor plans to expand its imaging satellite fleet by adding small satellites to enhance revisit rates. The new architecture aims to combine high resolution with rapid revisit capabilities.

title: Apex to develop larger satellites for missile defense, space-based computing
summary: Apex is developing larger satellites named 'Comet Mini' and 'Comet XL' to support energy-intensive missions like missile defense and space-based computing. The company aims to address high-power demands in these critical areas.

title: Watch Artemis 2 race back to Earth in this telescope livestream tonight
summary: A Virtual Telescope Project livestream will track NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft as it returns to Earth. The event can be watched live tonight.

title: 1 week until the Lyrid meteor shower lights up April skies: Here's what you need to know
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower will peak overnight on April 21-22, illuminating the skies. Here's what you need to know about this celestial event.

title: NASA astronaut Leland Melvin makes a cartoon cameo in latest episode of Disney+'s 'Hey A.J.!'
summary: NASA astronaut Leland Melvin made a cartoon cameo in the latest episode of Disney+'s 'Hey A.J.' He shared a motivational message about dreaming big.

title: Artemis 2 astronaut poses for epic selfie | Space photo of the day for April 9, 2026
summary: Astronaut Christina Koch took an epic selfie inside the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft, featuring a zero-gravity indicator named 'Rise'. The image was highlighted as Space photo of the day for April 9, 2026.

title: 'We all pretty much broke down right there': Inside the Artemis 2 astronauts' emotional moment near the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts experienced an emotional moment near the moon after breaking the human spaceflight distance record, strengthening their bond. This milestone highlights the mission's significance in advancing lunar exploration.

title: Light pollution has brightened Earth by 16% since 2014, satellites find
summary: Artificial lights at night have increased Earth's brightness by 16% from 2014 to 2022, according to a new satellite-based study.

title: Artemis 2 mission sends 'Kerbal Space Program' player numbers soaring to the Mun
summary: The upcoming Artemis 2 mission has significantly boosted interest in the 'Kerbal Space Program' game, with players inspired to attempt lunar missions. The mission highlights NASA's lunar ambitions, encouraging public engagement in space exploration.

title: All eyes on Orion’s heat shield: Artemis 2 astronauts will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10
summary: Artemis 2's Orion capsule will re-enter Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10, relying on a heat shield and parachutes for a safe splashdown. The mission marks a critical test for NASA's Artemis program ahead of future lunar landings.

title: Large Hadron Collider gives scientists their best look yet at conditions right after the Big Bang
summary: The ALICE experiment at CERN's Large Hadron Collider has provided scientists with unprecedented insights into quark-gluon plasma, the primordial matter present shortly after the Big Bang. This breakthrough enhances understanding of the early universe's conditions.

title: Twin NASA Control Rooms Support Artemis Safety, Success
summary: NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center is supporting Artemis II mission operations with two control rooms to ensure astronaut safety and mission success as the crew prepares to return to Earth. The LUCA and LESA control areas are actively involved in real-time lunar orbit activities.

title: Solar Eclipse of the Heart
summary: Artemis II astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft captured a unique solar eclipse from their vantage point during a lunar flyby in April 2026. The event was only visible to them due to their position in space.

title: Celestial Wonders in Leo
summary: Leo is a prominent constellation in April, featuring the bright star Regulus and a distinctive sickle shape. It offers celestial delights like a double star and a galactic trio for stargazers.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew shared the first flyby images of the Moon, revealing previously unseen regions and a rare in-space solar eclipse. The photos were released on April 8, 2026, following ongoing scientific observations.

title: Earthset
summary: The Artemis II crew captured images of Earth setting from lunar orbit on April 6, 2026, while documenting the Moon's geological features. The mission provided detailed observations of impact craters, lava flows, and surface cracks.

title: Experience the Rollout of SLS Hardware for Artemis III
summary: NASA invites digital creators and social media users to witness the rollout of the SLS core stage for Artemis III from Michoud Assembly Facility to Kennedy Space Center.

title: NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon
summary: NASA will host a media call with the Artemis II crew on April 8, 2026, at 10:45 p.m. EDT, allowing journalists to speak with the astronauts during their return journey from the Moon. The 20-minute event follows an update to the original advisory.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing efforts to support future launch operations.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission progresses.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is progressing with Orion spacecraft approaching a historic lunar flyby. This marks a critical mid-flight phase for the mission.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humans to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets, with liftoffs planned from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory, an American company, is contributing to NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the growing role of private industry in advancing space exploration.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit aboard the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved accuracy or new methods for orientation control. It may highlight a specific company or organization leading these innovations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's 86th parabolic flight campaign. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials efficiently, with potential applications for solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functionalities, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. This breakthrough could enhance satellite and spacecraft systems with advanced computing capabilities.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and communicating again.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities, thematic priorities, and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will be launched on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration and technology.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has closed a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investment from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch discusses how launch constraints and deadlines can influence competition in the space industry, citing examples like Golden Dome. The State of Launch 2026 report highlights these capability gates as critical factors for eligibility.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. This marks a significant advancement in the company's satellite technology portfolio.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The 'State of ISAM 2026' report highlights the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) as it evolves beyond a single concept. The article emphasizes the expanding scope and challenges of ISAM technologies and applications.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-made radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. The technology serves as a warning system for potential solar radiation threats.
company: Czech space industry (likely involving HERA or related entities)

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: Advocates and lawmakers are preparing to oppose NASA's proposed $18.8 billion budget, which includes significant cuts to science and STEM missions. This marks a recurring conflict as previous budget proposals faced similar resistance.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with Activate Capital and Shield Capital co-leading the investment.

title: The State of EO 2026
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) industry is experiencing growth as more entities seek to deploy their own satellite capabilities. The trend is expected to continue through 2026.

title: How Earthlings Got Views of the Lunar Fly By
summary: Redwire provided 11 cameras for the Orion capsule, enabling live views during its lunar flyby. These cameras were installed both inside and outside the spacecraft.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. Eiichi Yonekura, the company's President & CEO, discussed these plans in a recent interview.

title: FCC to Vote on Order to Update EPFD Framework
summary: The FCC is set to vote on an order to update the EPFD framework, which governs spectrum-sharing rules between Geostationary (GEO) and Non-Geostationary (NGSO) satellite systems. The proposed changes aim to replace existing regulations.

title: Italy and Argentina to Enhance Space Collaboration With Telespazio and VENG Deal
summary: Italy and Argentina have agreed to strengthen their space collaboration through a new multi-year deal between e-GEOS (a joint venture of the Italian Space Agency and Telespazio) and VENG. The agreement focuses on enhancing space data cooperation.

title: Northrop Grumman to Build HUGEO Satellite for Hungary’s 4iG
summary: Northrop Grumman will build Hungary's first communications satellite, HUGEO, for 4iG Space and Defense Technologies. The deal marks a significant step for Hungary's space capabilities.

title: April/May Magazine: Space as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Via Satellite's April/May magazine focuses on space as critical infrastructure and cybersecurity challenges for lunar activities. It also covers Europe's increase in space defense efforts.

title: It’s Unanimous: Space Already Functions as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Experts agree that space functions as critical infrastructure in the U.S., even without official designation. The sector is deeply integrated into government, academia, and industry operations.

title: Cybersecurity in Space is Hard; In Cislunar Space, it’s Really Hard
summary: The article discusses the challenges of cybersecurity in space, highlighting that traditional terrestrial cybersecurity strategies are difficult to apply in space and even more so in cislunar space. It emphasizes the complexity of protecting space-based networks and systems.

title: In-Space Mobility, Maturing From Last Miles to Superhighways
summary: NASA studied reusable 'space tug' systems in the late 1960s as part of the Space Transportation System (STS). The concept is now evolving into more advanced in-space mobility solutions.

title: Is Europe Moving Fast Enough to Close its Space Defense Gaps?
summary: Europe is accelerating efforts to address its space defense gaps amid concerns over Russia's military buildup and potential threats to NATO within four years. The urgency to strengthen space capabilities is driving rapid policy and technological responses.

title: Space Is the Best Offset Strategy
summary: The article discusses space as a crucial national security offset strategy, leveraging technological advantages to counter adversarial strengths. It highlights the role of space in modern defense and deterrence.
